title: musicTrack1/2/3Play alCSPGetState busy-wait spin — latent hang landmine on rapid track transitions
repro: grep tree for alCSPGetState spin; our synth is synchronous per frame (portAudioFrame) so spin either passes immediately or deadlocks — since music works, likely former, but latent for rapid transitions

history.note: Survey F4. GoldenRecomp hung two ways (clang collapsed empty spin -> optnone; spin starved cooperative scheduler -> yield_self_1ms). 5-min audit: classify, comment, bound if present.

AUDITED (survey F4): the alCSPGetState busy-wait (music.c:1237/1510/1780, x3 copy-paste in musicTrack1/2/3Play) is DEAD CODE in NATIVE_PORT — the synth block returns at music.c:1234 before the spin (N64-path body follows). GoldenRecomp's hazard (spin starves cooperative scheduler / clang collapses empty spin) does NOT apply: native never reaches it. Latent-hang concern REFUTED for the native build. Defensive comment added at the primary site documenting the guard invariant + the bounded retail pattern (music.c:291 for attempts<8) to bound it IF the native early-return is ever removed. Comment-only, byte-identical.
